title: Actinostemon lasiocarpus
taxonomy: Actinostemon lasiocarpus is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The genus Actinostemon is part of the Euphorbiaceae family, which comprises a large number of species commonly known as spurge or euphorbia. Actinostemon lasiocarpus is native to South America and is a small shrub or tree. The classification of Actinostemon lasiocarpus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. At the rank of order, it is classified as Malpighiales, a large and diverse order within the Euphorbiaceae family.

intro: Actinostemon lasiocarpus, commonly known as the 'fire tree,' is a plant species native to the tropical forests of South America, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Actinostemon lasiocarpus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which have been isolated and studied for their potential therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a salve or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
